description: Quality delta between Claude without the skill vs with the skill loaded

typical_issues: applyTo pattern absent or set to '**' when file-specific was requested
Rules use 'should' / 'prefer' language that is unverifiable
File placed at root with no location guidance
Rule set is verbose and includes explanatory prose

improvements: All rules rewritten in imperative, verifiable form
Concise variant selected based on rule complexity

scenarios.delta_explanation: applyTo precision and imperative language are the two most common gaps; skill addresses both.

typical_issues: Vague rule emitted unchanged ('write good, clean code')
Rule added to instruction file without evaluation

improvements: Vague rule rejected with explanation of why it is unverifiable
Clarifying questions asked to extract intent

scenarios.delta_explanation: Without skill, vague rules are silently accepted and pollute instruction files.

typical_issues: Contradicting rule silently added to instruction file
Both conflicting rules present in output — undefined behavior for Claude

improvements: Conflict detection step in workflow prompts a check
Only the winning rule is emitted after resolution

scenarios.delta_explanation: Silent contradictions are harmful and hard to debug. Skill prevents them entirely.
